jobPostingInfo.title: Software Engineering Intern
jobPostingInfo.jobDescription:
When you join Verizon

You want more out of a career. A place to share your ideas freely — even if they’re daring or different. Where the true you can learn, grow, and thrive. At Verizon, we power and empower how people live, work and play by connecting them to what brings them joy. We do what we love — driving innovation, creativity, and impact in the world. Our V Team is a community of people who anticipate, lead, and believe that listening is where learning begins. In crisis and in celebration, we come together — lifting our communities and building trust in how we show up, everywhere & always. Want in? Join the #VTeamLife.

When you join Verizon

Verizon is a leading provider of technology, communications, information and entertainment products, transforming the way we connect across the globe. We’re a diverse network of people driven by our ambition and united in our shared purpose to shape a better future. Here, we have the ability to learn and grow at the speed of technology, and the space to create within every role. Together, we are moving the world forward – and you can too. Dream it. Build it. Do it here.

What you’ll be doing...

We are looking for ambitious students to join our 2026 internship program. You will be guided through a 6 month program in R&D.

  • Be part of a cross functional team, gaining exposure to Lean Agile methodologies, Software Development and Design, Testing, DevOps and CI/CD to name a few.

  • Learn from industry experts in technologies such as AWS, Angular, C#/.Net, and disciplines like Machine Learning, UX, User Research, Product management.

  • Have the opportunity to work on real projects from start to finish.

  • Have a designated mentor throughout the program to guide you along the way.

  • Have the opportunity to gain a wealth of knowledge and experience as you work alongside our highly skilled engineers.

What we’re looking for...

You’ll need to have:

  • Currently pursuing a Bachelors or Master’s Degree or recently completed a Bachelors or Master’s Degree in Computer Science, Software Engineering, or equivalent education.

  • Knowledge and/or experience in areas such as Object Oriented Programming, Web Development, Unit Testing, Software Design Patterns, Algorithms and Data Structures.

  • Programming experience with C#, Java, Python or relevant programming languages.

  • Fluency in English.

Even better if you have:

  • Projects that showcase your work - both University curriculum and personal projects.

  • A strong work ethic and willingness to learn, with demonstrated experience of that.

  • The ability to work well when given instruction but can also work autonomously.

  • Previous work experience, related or unrelated to your degree, or volunteering experience.

  • Excellent interpersonal skills, you are motivated and have a positive attitude to work.

Where you’ll be working

In this hybrid role, you'll have a defined work location that includes work from home and assigned office days set by your manager.

Scheduled Weekly Hours

39

Equal Employment Opportunity

Verizon is an equal opportunity employer. We evaluate qualified applicants without regard to race, gender, disability or any other legally protected characteristics.
